non_blocking_recommendations: Add an explicit CLI regression test that --target, --url, --host, --scope, and --live are rejected by argparse, even though the current parser only defines --fixture, --run-id, and --policy-decision-sha256.
Consider replacing or supplementing regex-based static safety tests with AST-based import/call checks so future formatting changes cannot evade forbidden import/write detection.
Keep future work separated from P2-16: runtime finding/evidence emission, runner wiring, schema bumps, or live target support should require a new T3 review.

notes: Reviewed modules/checks/level1/security_headers_baseline/check.py, module.json, README.md, scripts/test_security_headers_baseline.py, fixtures, triage docs, and P2-16 handoff direction/task files.

check.py imports only argparse, json, re, sys, pathlib.Path, and typing.Any; no socket, DNS, HTTP client, urllib, requests, httpx, aiohttp, ssl, subprocess, process, thread, async, or callback implementation was found in the module.
Runtime module path reads the fixture file and prints a single-line JSON array to stdout; no filesystem write APIs were found in check.py.
CLI exposes only --fixture, --run-id, and --policy-decision-sha256; no live target flags were implemented.
module.json declares requires_network=false, network_access=none, target_touching=false, destructive=false, intrusive=false, emits_findings=false, and emits_evidence=false.
Searches found no security_headers_baseline wiring in module_runner, config, .hermes.md, bin, or YAML workflow files.

Target values in committed fixtures are constrained by tests to reserved/lab placeholders. Findings remain candidate status with scanner_output_only and manual_verification_required true and evidence empty.

No P2-16 schema bump or preview/runtime emission path was observed in the reviewed module files. The working tree contains other unrelated modified/untracked files from adjacent phases, but no P2-16 runner/recon/config wiring was found.

gaps: No explicit test currently asserts argparse rejection of --target/--url/--host/--scope/--live, although current behavior rejects unknown flags by default.
Static forbidden import/write tests are regex-based rather than AST-based; current source passes, but AST checks would be more robust.
Redaction assertions focus on rendered finding fields and triage drafts; structured target.value remains present in the finding target object as required by the finding shape.

validated_value: Implemented exactly one offline Level 1 module, level1.security_headers_baseline, for fixture-driven workflow validation.

Closed fixture shape, deterministic evaluate() output, six input/golden fixture pairs, and six triage drafts plus README validate the intended committed-fixture to candidate-finding to human-triage path.
Candidate findings validate against existing finding/1.0 via the test suite, with empty evidence and manual verification gates preserved.
Local validation run passed: py_compile plus python -m unittest scripts.test_security_headers_baseline -v ran 14 tests OK.
Module manifest validation passed with verdict allow.
remaining_uncertainty: The module is not wired into runtime execution by design, so this validates the offline workflow chain rather than operational module_runner behavior.
Future evidence/finding emission semantics remain intentionally deferred and unvalidated in P2-16.
The repository working tree includes unrelated adjacent-phase changes, so final integration review should keep P2-16 scope isolated when committing.
workflow_value_assessment.recommended_next_step: Accept P2-16 as an offline workflow-validation slice, then defer any runtime emission, runner integration, schema changes, or live target handling to a separate T3-reviewed phase.
